description_blurb: The FBI's 62-HQ-83894 case file includes investigative records, eyewitness testimonies, and public reports concerning Unidentified Flying Objects and flying discs documented between June 1947 and July 1968. The records include high-profile incident accounts, photographic evidence from sites like Oak Ridge, TN, and technical proposals regarding potential propulsion systems. Additional topics include convention programs, researcher accounts, and extensive media coverage from the period.  This file is partially posted on FBI vault with more redactions and some pages missing.  Included here is the complete case file with several newly declassified pages and only minor redactions.
title: 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_7
incident_location: Savannah River Plant, AEC; Jackson, Minnesota
summary: This section of the FBI case file 62-HQ-83894 contains various communications and reports concerning Unidentified Flying Objects (UFOs) and flying discs from July-August 1952. Key incidents include two Du Pont employees observing a blue light with an orange fringe, shaped like a saucer, flying at high speed over the Savannah River Plant. Another report details a 'steady white light' circling a farm in Jackson, Minnesota, followed by the discovery of 'tinsel'-like material and burned ground in a triangular pattern. The document also includes a translated anonymous German communication claiming flying saucers are V-weapons in Russian hands, capable of carrying atom bombs, and reports from the Borderland Sciences Research Associates claiming communication with 'men operating the flying saucers' who are on a peaceful mission but possess 'disintegrators' for defense. The FBI's general response to these reports was to forward them to the Air Force, stating no further action was contemplated by the Bureau.
reported_object_description: blue light with an orange fringe shaped like a saucer; object flying at a high rate of speed; steady white light; round body similar to a disk having a diameter of about 48-50 m; outer ring with about 45-50 automatic circular nozzles; plexiglass sphere in the center; tinsel-like material
reported_behavior: flying at a high rate of speed; traveling in a northeastern direction; circling their farm; soaring around in the atmosphere; fast getaway up into the sky at a very fast speed; disintegrate planes completely
official_resolution: Referred to United States Air Force; No further action by FBI; FBI determined no violation within its jurisdiction
bust_reasoning: The Savannah River sighting is a single-sensor visual report of a light with subjective shape and speed. The Jackson, Minnesota description, as detailed in the metadata, includes highly fantastical elements (e.g., specific number of nozzles, 'disintegrate planes') that strongly suggest an imaginative account or fabrication rather than a factual observation. The German letter is an anonymous speculative theory about a V-weapon, not a direct observation of a UAP. No multi-sensor corroboration or features resisting mundane explanations are present.

August 11, 1952

Mr. Edmond J. Kane
Mauston, Wisconsin
[Handwritten in red, crossed out with blue X: 62-83894-303]

Dear Mr. Kane:

Your letter dated August 1, 1952, has been received, and I appreciate the interest which prompted your bringing your observations to my attention.

Inasmuch as the matter of the flying saucers is being investigated by the United States Air Force, I am taking the liberty of forwarding a copy of your letter to the Director of Special Investigations, The Inspector General, Department of the Air Force, The Pentagon, Washington, D. C. If you have further observations along this line, I would suggest that you may wish to communicate directly with him.

Sincerely yours,

John Edgar Hoover
Director

Copy by form to Air Force Intelligence.

NOTE: Special Agent Samuel Hardy, EOD 3-28-25, GS-12, $8760, assigned to Minneapolis.
Special Agent Clinton W. Stein, EOD 4-6-31, GS-15, $10,800, assigned Division II as inspector.

# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_7 - Page 7

Mauston, Wisconsin, 1 August 1952

Mr. J. Edgar Hoover, Director
FBI
Washington, D. C.

(y)

Dear Sir:

O Flying SAUCERS

As a retired member of the St Paul Police Force and having had numerous pleasant contacts with Agent Clintonstein and Samy Hardy, I take the liberty of an opinion that came to me that the flying saucers scare seems possible that the released energy of exploded Atomic Bombs could be soaring around in the atmosphere. I know you can channel this thought to the proper authorities and certainly would appreciate your comments on this thought.

I am a Veteran of both World War #1 and #2 and also a member of the American Legion for 32 years. I am presently a member of The American Legion Post #81 of Mauston, Wisconsin which is known as the Burton-Koppang Post.

If this thought is instrumental in tracking down the source of this scare thereby releaving some of the tension to our American Country, it may be worth following up at this time.

Thanking you very much for your cooperation and consideration in this matter and will you please acknowledge and advise your comments.

Yours very truly, es

Edmond J. Kane
Edmond J. Kane
Mauston, Wisconsin

**DATE:** August 8, 1952

Reference is made to a letter from the Cincinnati Office dated August 6, 1952, submitting an anonymous letter in German concerning so-called "Flying Saucers." [Handwritten in right margin: Brathigan / Mirsbury / Ehn]

Attached are 7 copies of a translation of this German material prepared in the Cryptanalysis-Translation Section.

No reply has been made by us to the August 6, 1952, letter from Cincinnati.

RECOMMENDATION:

That the Domestic Intelligence Division review the Cincinnati submission and accompanying Bureau translation, for appropriate handling, noting particularly a suggestion in the last paragraph of the Cincinnati letter to consider making this information available to other Governmental Agencies. [Handwritten in right margin: cle]

# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_7 - Page 20

TRANSLATION FROM GERMAN

Flying Saucer,
a weapon tested in 1944, which is probably now being produced in series, and at this time causes a great stir, is a V-weapon which has a round body similar to a disk having a diameter of about 48-50 m. On the outer ring it has about 45-50 automatic circular nozzles which after the ignition of the disk circulate around a plexiglass sphere in the center in which the measuring and controlling devices for long-distance steering are located. In the sphere is still sufficient space for atom bombs. These weapons are in Russian hands and can have an effective range of 30-35,000 km. The constructor of V-weapons RIEDEL in Germany stated that it concerns a typical V-weapon on which he had worked himself. I am sure that the truth is better than a panic among ignorant people.

H. SCH.

"TRANSLATED BY:
FRIEDRICH G. NEUHAUSER: bad
AUGUST 8, 1952."

[Bottom Left]
COPIES DESTROYED
270 NOV 19 1964

[Bottom Right]
[Faint stamp]

Mr. William Hoffmeyer
Route 3
Jackson, Minnesota

Dear Mr. Hoffmeyer:

Your letter postmarked August 15, 1952, together with enclosure, has been received, and I want to thank you for bringing this matter to my attention.

Inasmuch as the contents of your communication do not reflect any violation within the jurisdiction of this Bureau, I am unable to comply with your request.

I am taking the liberty of forwarding a copy of your letter and your original enclosure to the Director of Special Investigations, The Inspector General, Department of the Air Force, The Pentagon, Washington 25, D. C., since these matters may be of interest to that agency.

Sincerely yours,

John Edgar Hoover
Director

# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_7 - Page 29

TRUE COPY

Jackson Minn
Aug 2, 1952

J. Edgar Hoover
Federal bureau of Investigation.
Washington, D. C.

Dear Mr Hoover:

I don't know if you analyze articles so will send you a sample of (something that looks like tinsel) because my uncle found it in a pasture in a round pile, underneath of it, it looks as tho the clover was burned. & there are 3 similar patches which are burned in a triangle form. Last Sunday Morning the Neighbors Wife got up to get a drink of water & happned to see a steady white Light circling their farm, but didn't pay any attention to it. Than Sunday morn. when my uncle went to get the cows, he saw this stuff shining in the Sun light. It couldn't of been dropped out of a plane as it was loose & not in a container & the nearest road is about a ½ mile from the pasture & now we are wondering what it is. So would like to have you analyze it & let me know.

Sincerely yours,

/s/ Wm Hoffmeyer

Dear Mr. Lang:

EX. - 73 [Left margin]
Your letter of August 12, 1952, has been received and I want you to know that I appreciate the interest prompting you to bring your observations to my attention.

Inasmuch as the matter to which you refer is of interest to the United States Air Force authorities, I am taking the liberty of making a copy of your communication available to The Honorable, The Secretary of the Air Force, The Pentagon, Washington 25, D. C.

Sincerely yours,

John Edgar Hoover
Director

# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_7 - Page 33

Director, FBI
August 5, 1952
SAC, San Diego

BORDERLAND SCIENCES, RESEARCH ASSOCIATES
San Diego, California; MEAD LAYNE, Director;
MAX FREEDOM LONG, Director

In view of the current hysteria concerning the so-called "flying saucers", I thought that the Bureau might be interested in the following information concerning the captioned organization.

I am transmitting herewith certain literature concerning the captioned organization which was furnished to me by FRANCIS OHM, who operates the Businessmens Assurance Company in San Diego and who has his office in the same building housing the FBI office.

It is of interest to note that on Friday, August 1, 1952, at approximately 12:00 noon, FRANCIS OHM asked to see me urgently, which I did. As a member of the captioned organization, he advised me that they were having a meeting at 1:00 PM the same day, inasmuch as they had been advised that they had some very important information to discuss. He went on to relate that through the means utilized by his organization (which the enclosed literature will reflect borders on the occult), they were able to prognosticate well in advance the recent earthquake which occurred in Tehachapi, California. He advised that they have also been able to obtain the following information:

1. That a very severe tidal wave will hit the Pacific Ocean sometime in the not too remote future and that most of the Japanese Islands will be washed away and disappear.

2. That all fishes with scales in Japanese waters will soon leave these waters for other destinations, in view of the oncoming tidal wave.

3. That this tidal wave will wash away the Hawaiian Islands.

4. That the West Coast of the United States will likewise be affected by this tidal wave.

5. That they prognosticated the appearance of a new island in the Pacific which was published in the newspapers within the past week.

JFS:ha

[Left Margin]
ENCL.
[Handwritten: filed with original]

That the flying saucers are not fantasies; that they are factual and actual; that his associates in the captioned organization have been in conversation with the men operating the flying saucers, and that they have asked that high officials of the U. S. Government be informed that they are here on our planet on a peaceful mission and that they are not on a warlike mission. However, if the U. S. Government continues to send planes after these flying saucers and if these planes fire on the flying saucers, they have disintegrators which they will utilize and which will disintegrate these planes completely in no time flat.

Mr. OHM requested that either I myself attend the meeting at 1:00 PM or that I designate a stenographer to attend the meeting, so that she could take down all the minutes of the meeting. I told him that unfortunately this was very short notice, that I had another commitment and that our stenographers were pretty well occupied at that time. He said he would advise me of anything important that might transpire at the meeting. As of August 2, 1952, he has not advised me of anything which transpired at the meeting.

I should like to point out that OHM appears to be a perfectly sane, sound individual; he operates a very large insurance business in San Diego, and is convinced in his own mind of th
cover_up_reasoning: The document consists mainly of FBI administrative correspondence acknowledging receipt of UAP reports and forwarding them to the Air Force, stating no further FBI action. There are no black-bar redactions of substantive information, and the 'CONFIDENTIAL' marking on one page is struck through, indicating declassification. Illegibility on some pages appears to be due to poor scanning quality rather than intentional concealment.
summary_zh: 联邦调查局案件档案62-HQ-83894的本节包含有关不明飞行物（UFO）和飞碟的各类通信与报告，时间为1952年7月至8月。主要事件包括两名杜邦公司员工观察到碟形蓝色光束，带有橙色边缘，高速飞越萨凡纳河工厂。另一份报告详述了明尼苏达州杰克逊市一农场上空盘旋的“稳定白光”，随后发现“金属箔片”状物质和三角形烧焦地面。该文件还包含一份匿名德语通信译文，声称飞碟是俄罗斯掌握的V型武器，能够携带原子弹，以及边境科学研究协会的报告，声称与“操作飞碟的人”进行了通信，这些人执行和平任务但拥有用于防御的“分解器”。联邦调查局对这些报告的普遍回应是将其转交美国空军，并声明该局不打算采取进一步行动。
reported_object_description_zh: 碟形蓝色光束，带有橙色边缘；高速飞行物体；稳定白光；直径约48-50米的圆形碟状物体；带有约45-50个自动圆形喷嘴的外环；中央的有机玻璃球体；金属箔片状物质
reported_behavior_zh: 高速飞行；向东北方向移动；在其农场上空盘旋；在大气层中翱翔；以极快速度迅速升空逃离；完全分解飞机
official_resolution_zh: 转交美国空军；联邦调查局未采取进一步行动；联邦调查局认定无违反其管辖权范围内的行为
bust_reasoning_zh: 萨凡纳河目击事件是单一传感器视觉报告，描述了具有主观形状和速度的光源。明尼苏达州杰克逊市的描述，如元数据所述，包含高度奇幻的元素（例如，具体数量的喷嘴，“分解飞机”），强烈表明其为想象性叙述或捏造，而非事实观察。该德语信件是关于V型武器的匿名推测性理论，并非对UAP的直接观察。不存在多传感器佐证或抵制常规解释的特征。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件主要包含联邦调查局的行政往来函件，确认收到UAP报告并将其转交空军，声明联邦调查局不再采取进一步行动。没有对实质性信息进行黑条遮盖，且其中一页上的“CONFIDENTIAL”（机密）标记已被划掉，表明已解密。部分页面模糊不清似乎是由于扫描质量不佳，而非故意隐瞒。
incident_location_zh: 萨凡纳河工厂，美国原子能委员会；明尼苏达州杰克逊市
claims: Two employees observed a blue light with an orange fringe, shaped like a saucer, flying at high speed over the Savannah River Plant.
A white light was seen circling a farm, and 'tinsel'-like material and burned ground in a triangular form were found.
Flying saucers are V-weapons, tested in 1944, now in Russian hands, with a round body 48-50m in diameter, 45-50 circular nozzles, and a range of 30-35,000 km, capable of carrying atom bombs.
Flying saucers are factual and actual, operated by men on a peaceful mission.
The 'men operating the flying saucers' have disintegrators and will use them if US Government planes fire on flying saucers.
claims_zh: 两名员工观察到碟形蓝色光束，带有橙色边缘，高速飞越萨凡纳河工厂。
观察到一束白光在农场上空盘旋，并发现“金属箔片”状物质和三角形烧焦地面。
飞碟是V型武器，于1944年测试，现由俄罗斯掌握，其圆形主体直径48-50米，有45-50个圆形喷嘴，射程30-35,000公里，能够携带原子弹。
飞碟是真实存在的，由执行和平任务的人员操作。
“操作飞碟的人”拥有分解器，如果美国政府飞机向飞碟开火，他们将使用这些分解器。
witnesses: Two employees of E. I. Du Pont Company
bust_explanations: Witness misinterpretation or fabrication: The highly detailed and fantastical description from Jackson, Minnesota (e.g., '48-50m diameter,' '45-50 automatic circular nozzles,' 'plexiglass sphere,' 'tinsel-like material,' 'disintegrate planes completely') strongly suggests an imaginative account, a hoax, or extreme misinterpretation rather than a factual observation of physical characteristics.

Misidentified aircraft or meteor: The Savannah River sighting, described as a 'blue light with an orange fringe shaped like a saucer' flying at a 'high rate of speed,' is a classic single-sensor visual report that could easily be a misidentified conventional aircraft, a bright meteor, or other atmospheric phenomena.

Anonymous speculative theory: The German letter is not an observation of a UAP but rather an anonymous theoretical explanation for 'flying saucers' as a German V-weapon now in Russian hands. This is a mundane explanation for the document's content (a speculative letter), not for a UAP sighting itself.
bust_explanations_zh: 目击者误解或捏造：明尼苏达州杰克逊市的高度详细且奇幻的描述（例如，“直径48-50米”，“45-50个自动圆形喷嘴”，“有机玻璃球体”，“金属箔片状物质”，“完全分解飞机”）强烈表明其为想象性叙述、骗局或极端误解，而非对物理特征的事实观察。
误认飞机或流星：萨凡纳河目击事件被描述为“碟形蓝色光束，带有橙色边缘”，以“高速”飞行，这是一份典型的单一传感器视觉报告，很容易被误认为常规飞机、明亮流星或其他大气现象。
匿名推测性理论：该德语信件并非对UAP的观察，而是关于“飞碟”是德国V型武器现由俄罗斯掌握的匿名理论解释。这是对文件内容（一封推测性信件）的常规解释，而非对UAP目击事件本身的解释。
